result:
**Goal: Add a Planetary Accretion & Solar System Formation simulation mode.**

Simulate the birth of a solar system from a protoplanetary disk: dust and gas particles coalesce into planetesimals via gravitational accretion, clear their orbital lanes, undergo giant impacts and mergers, with the frost line determining rocky vs. icy composition. Watch inner rocky planets and outer gas giants self-organize from chaos — orbital resonances, planetary migration, moon capture, and a Late Heavy Bombardment phase. Three views: top-down orbital disk map with particle density and forming protoplanets, cross-section showing disk thickness/temperature/frost line, and sparkline time series tracking planet count, largest body mass, disk mass remaining, orbital eccentricities, collision rate, etc. Presets like Calm Accretion, Jupiter Migration (Grand Tack), Giant Impact (Moon formation), Resonant Chain (TRAPPIST-1 analog), Hot Jupiter Inward Spiral, and Late Heavy Bombardment.

This fills a gap between the existing Solar System Orrery (finished planets) and Galaxy Formation (galactic scale) — the missing middle chapter of how planets actually form from dust.
